commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs human maintainer acceptance of the default-off and one-worker imported-cluster rollout; no narrow automated repair remains.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the PR does not add dependencies, action refs, secrets, or permission broadening. (sha=e553ea52227a841fafc3712d562f4a9d77bf2c6d)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a default-off gitcrawl cluster and low-signal repair feature gate, gitcrawl-store lookup preference, a one-worker cluster repair capacity lane, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature and rollout-policy PR, not a bug report. Source review plus the successful head checks are the relevant validation path.
commands.review_followup: Needs human maintainer acceptance of the default-off and one-worker imported-cluster rollout; no narrow automated repair remains.

target.title: feat: gate migrated cluster repair lane
target.body:
## Summary
- Migrate the Clownfish-style cluster repair lane into ClawSweeper using gitcrawl/gitcrawl-store cluster data.
- Keep it separate from the existing issue implementation solver: issue implementation handles individually triaged strict reproducible bugs, while cluster repair handles imported issue/PR clusters and low-signal PR cleanup candidates.
- Gate only the migrated cluster lane behind `CLAWSWEEPER_FEATURE_CLUSTER_REPAIR_ENABLED=1`; existing comment-router, autofix/automerge repair, issue implementation, commit-finding, finalizer, requeue, and self-heal paths keep their existing gates.
- Run that cluster feature gate before gitcrawl source detection, SQLite reads, cluster selection, or output directory writes for non-plan imports.
- Drip-feed imported gitcrawl cluster work by default: cluster import skips already-imported, security-sensitive, feature-request, and 75%+ closed clusters unless `--skip-closed-percent` is explicitly overridden.
- Preserve the existing repair/automerge/issue-implementation 40%-of-`workers.max` capacity derivation; only imported gitcrawl cluster and low-signal PR jobs use the new one-worker `cluster_repair` default lane.
- Reuse the existing repair worker/prompt/execute stack with `job_intent`-aware filtering so migrated cluster jobs do not affect existing ClawSweeper repair behavior.
- Prefer portable `gitcrawl-store` SQLite state before the legacy local gitcrawl DB.
- Preserve explicit `CLAWSWEEPER_MAX_LIVE_WORKERS` / `--max-live-workers` override headroom up to the configured global worker cap.

## Enablement
To enable the migrated cluster lane in a target repo, add the Actions repository variable `CLAWSWEEPER_FEATURE_CLUSTER_REPAIR_ENABLED=1`.

Live mutation still also requires the existing gates, mainly `CLAWSWEEPER_ALLOW_EXECUTE=1` and `CLAWSWEEPER_ALLOW_FIX_PR=1`. Merge behavior remains separately gated by `CLAWSWEEPER_ALLOW_MERGE=1` where applicable.

## Audit Notes
- `docs/repair/README.md` keeps both examples: normal repair dispatch at the current 22-worker derived default and imported cluster dispatch at the 1-worker cluster default.
- `scripts/check-limits.ts` now verifies both examples, so the cluster docs check complements rather than replaces the normal repair docs check.
- `README.md` and `docs/limits.md` describe existing repair capacity as 40% of `workers.max`, not a fixed checked-in 22.

## Verification
- `pnpm run check`
- `pnpm run check:limits`
target.author: romneyda

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
